title: Bug when converting json with netnames to blif
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Execute the following Yosys script incurs unexpected behavior:
```json5
read_json <<EOH
{
  "modules": {
    "m": {
      "ports": {
        "A": { "direction": "input", "bits": [ 2 ] },
        "Y": { "direction": "output", "bits": [ 2 ] }
      },
      "cells": { },
      "netnames": {
        "A": { "hide_name": 0, "bits": [ 2 ] },
        "Y": { "hide_name": 0, "bits": [ 2 ] }
      }
    }
  }
}
EOH
write_blif
```

Note that `netnames` section is NECESSARY to reproduce the bug.

## Expected behavior

The correct blif should contain a single LUT connecting A and Y.
```blif
.model m
.inputs A
.outputs Y
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.names A Y
1 1
.end
```

## Actual behavior

Under `Yosys 0.9+4052 (git sha1 a5adb00, gcc 10.2.0 -fPIC -Os)`, there are two connections from A to Y.
```blif
.model m
.inputs A
.outputs Y
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.names A Y
1 1
.names A Y
1 1
.end
```

## Workaround

Remove the `netnames` section from JSON.
code_blocks:
.model m
.inputs A
.outputs Y
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.names A Y
1 1
.names A Y
1 1
.end
